commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A narrow mechanical repair can close the remaining falsey malformed-decision gap; maintainer review is still needed for the broader fail-closed compatibility choice.; Needs attention: The patch hardens a tool-execution policy boundary, but one malformed return class still fails open.; Review finding: [P1] Block falsey malformed trusted decisions — src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts:294-296 (sha=ee4b38fcb8e9b74236bd09a330ac330474ee9de2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR defensively reads trusted tool policy registrations and decisions, blocks on unreadable or throwing trusted-policy data, and adds focused contract tests for those failure modes.

PR surface: Source +202, Tests +148. Total +350 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: make a trusted policy return `false`, `null`, `0`, or `""`; the PR head reaches `if (!decision) continue` and skips the policy instead of blocking. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow mechanical repair can close the remaining falsey malformed-decision gap; maintainer review is still needed for the broader fail-closed compatibility choice.

target.title: fix(plugins): fail closed on trusted policy errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Fails closed when a plugin-owned trusted tool policy registration, evaluator, decision, or adjusted params are unreadable or throw.
- Keeps healthy trusted-policy chaining behavior intact for normal approvals, blocks, and adjusted params.
- Intentionally scoped to runtime trusted-policy execution. Broader fuzz/registration hardening remains out of scope for this PR.

## Linked context

Related #87141

Requested by maintainer as a pruned critical-hardening slice from the larger fuzzing branch.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: malformed or throwing trusted tool policy data can no longer fail open or silently skip the policy gate.
- Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper after final rebase; AWS Crabbox/Testbox changed gate before the final clean rebase.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts`
  - private-name/private-path branch scrub
  - `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
- Evidence after fix: focused contract test passed 55 tests after final rebase at head `ee4b38fcb8e9b74236bd09a330ac330474ee9de2`; oxfmt passed; diff check passed; final branch scrub found no private paths or private reported names; AWS Crabbox run `run_a78421d9ec7f` on lease `cbx_a6e3f576ac51` passed `pnpm check:changed` before the final clean rebase.
- Observed result after fix: thrown evaluators, unreadable registrations, malformed truthy decisions, unreadable decision fields, and unreadable adjusted params all return deterministic blocking results instead of allowing the tool call to continue.
- What was not tested: a live packaged third-party plugin invoking this path; broad changed gate was run before the final clean rebase, then the touched surface was rechecked with focused tests and formatting after rebase.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is a synthetic hardening regression test path using `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixtures, not the private reported plugin/tool names.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts`
- private-name/private-path branch scrub
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Regression coverage added:

- trusted policy evaluator throws
- unreadable trusted policy registration
- unreadable policy decision object
- malformed truthy primitive decision
- unreadable adjusted params

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed trusted policy plugin data now blocks tool calls instead of failing open.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This tightens tool execution policy handling at the trusted-policy boundary.

What is the highest-risk area?

A malformed trusted policy that previously no-oped now blocks tool execution with a deterministic reason.

How is that risk mitigated?

The behavior is limited to unreadable, throwing, or malformed plugin-owned policy data. Normal allow/block/approval/params chaining is covered by the existing contract tests plus the new fail-closed cases.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Draft PR for maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local structured review found one malformed truthy-decision fail-open gap; this PR now blocks that case and includes a regression test.
